text.fullText:
To whomever it may concern,

As a current public health graduate student; this proposal is important to me as I am highly interested in environmental health and its impact to all species of life. The concise focus on how water quality directly affects community health outcomes, highlights the nature of how important this proposal is and its revisions that are offered. In relation to clean water being the foundation for disease prevention and overalll population health, my interests in how federal policy influences real-world environmental protections play hand in hand. From my understanding, the proposal in general wants to update the Clean Water Act 401 water quality certification rules and regulations. The U.S. Environmental Protection Agency suggests that this new proposal on certification regulations improves numeorus goals set forth in different aspects such as clarity, efficiency and predictability in certification processing. Although the Clean Water Act covers all aspects associated with rules and regulations pertaining to the importance of keeping our water safe and protected from harmful impacts, the emphasis on reviewing discharges into waters of the United States is just as important; therefore we can see why making sure the process of receiving a water quality certificate are concise and accurate is equally important.
The reason I support this proposal is because of the clear processes and actions taken to help states or tribes make timely descisions that affect the health of our waters are both important. The introduction of over emphasizing efficient the elimination of environmental safeguards. The revisions that are being proposed for implementation to me seems to hold predicatliby that benefits the health of the public by ensuring consistent enforcement of water quality standards. Industrial pollution, air pollution, and water pollution have all caused reoccurring illnesses and health conditions among the population.
Directly focusing on water pollution, numeorus diseases such as but not limited to; gastrointestinal illness, chronic illnesses, and other environmental exposure risks are all related to the health of our waters. Environmental health will continue to be a major concern and contributor to our planets health and health of humanity. Regulations pertaining to environmental health matters to me because these priorities can and will help health protection, not just administrative efficiency. In accordance with this proposal, I want to suggest more advocation towards water pollution and the safety of the public. However, with the encouragement from EPA when it comes to maintaining a health centered approach when it comes to finalizing this rule will help push forward this proposal. With that being said, I want to extend my great appreciation for the opportunity to comment on the proposed rule and utilize my voice. All in all, I hope that the final say in this proposed rule will help continue the priority of clean water as a public health necessity and not dim the concerns that are proposed.
